name: Puebla de la Reina — Matachel River
name_original: Puebla de la Reina — circular por el río Matachel
description: Explore the Matachel River on this 30.5-mile moderate mountain bike loop from Puebla de la Reina, featuring diverse landscapes and spring bl
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Puebla De La Reina are situated in the province of Badajoz, Extremadura, Spain, offering a diverse landscape for outdoor activities. The region is characterized by its location at the foothills of the Sierra de Hornachos, providing a hilly and mountainous environment. Features like the Matachel River and ancient drover's roads contribute to varied terrain, including riverside paths and singletrack sections. This topography creates a range of routes suitable for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails in Puebla De La Reina The most popular mountain bike trail is Puebla de la Reina — Matachel River, a 30.5 miles (49.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 45 minutes to complete. This route follows the course of the Matachel River, offering scenic views and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sierra Grande de Hornachos and Matachel River Loop, a moderate 32.2 miles (51.8 km) path. This loop explores the foothills of the Sierra Grande de Hornachos, combining elevation with riverside sections. Local mountain bikers also love the Camino de la Umbría Trail, a 9.8 miles (15.8 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 50 minutes. Mountain biking around Puebla De La Reina is defined by the Sierra de Hornachos, the Matachel River, and ancient drover's roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate climbs to longer, more challenging loops. The routes in Puebla De La Reina are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 3 reviews. More than 60 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Puebla De La Reina's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Puebla De La Reina?
A: Puebla de la Reina offers a selection of over 10 mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. These routes explore the diverse landscapes at the foothills of the Sierra de Hornachos and along the Matachel River.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Puebla De La Reina?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail available. The region's trails are generally well-suited for a range of riders, with some featuring moderate climbs and easy-to-follow singletrack sections.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Puebla De La Reina?
A: The landscape around Puebla de la Reina changes beautifully throughout the year. Spring is particularly recommended when the region blooms, offering picturesque scenery along routes like those following the Matachel River. Winters can also be suitable, but it's always wise to check local trail conditions.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails with significant elevation gain for a challenge?
A: Absolutely. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24528293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Hornachos loop from Hornachos&lt;/a&gt; offer substantial elevation gain, with over 1300 meters of ascent, providing a challenging experience for advanced riders. The Sierra de Hornachos area generally provides a hilly and mountainous environment ideal for climbs.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Puebla De La Reina?
A: Yes, many of the trails in the area are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1275033356/sierra-grande-de-hornachos-and-matachel-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Grande de Hornachos and Matachel River Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route that combines elevation with scenic riverside sections.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the mountain bike trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. The ascent to the Mirador de la Navilla is a notable highlight, offering stunning views and an ideal spot for recreational activities. Many routes, especially those traversing the Sierra de Hornachos, will provide panoramic vistas.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I see near the mountain bike trails?
A: You can explore several interesting sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4146077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Pajosa&lt;/a&gt;, a castle rising on the slopes of the Sierra de la Utrera, offers historical interest and scenic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6046613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matachel River&lt;/a&gt; itself is a beautiful natural feature, and you might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matachel River Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly mountain bike routes in Puebla De La Reina?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on mountain bike trails can vary, the natural environment of Puebla de la Reina, with its extensive rural paths and drover's roads like the Cañada Real "Cabeza de Ciervo," generally offers opportunities for riding with well-behaved dogs. Always ensure your dog is under control and check local signage for any restrictions.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trailheads in Puebla De La Reina?
A: Puebla de la Reina is a small municipality, and parking is generally available within the town or at designated starting points for trails. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check the route details on komoot, as many tours provide information on convenient starting locations.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Puebla De La Reina?
A: The mountain bike trails around Puebla de la Reina are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from riverside paths to mountainous sections, and the scenic beauty of the Sierra de Hornachos and Matachel River.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the mountain bike routes in Puebla De La Reina?
A: Puebla de la Reina, as a local village, offers amenities including cafes and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your ride. It's a good idea to check local opening hours, especially outside of peak tourist seasons.

Q: Are there any permits required for mountain biking in the Extremadura region?
A: Generally, mountain biking on established trails and public rights of way in Extremadura does not require specific permits. However, if you plan to ride through private land or protected natural areas (like the ZEPA de Hornachos), it's always best to check for any specific regulations or restrictions that might apply.
tips: Puebla de la Reina is a small town in the Matachel river basin. Head to the Church of Santa Olalla and have a drink in the Plaza after your adventure.
Very nice path through the waters of the Matachel river. This landscape changes throughout the year depending on rainfall. This path blooms in spring after the rains.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the circular mountain bike route along the Matachel River?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 49.1 kilometers long and typically takes around 3 hours and 45 minutes to complete by mountain bike. This duration assumes a moderate pace and good physical fitness.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While the trail is officially classified as an 'Intermediate mountain bike ride' requiring 'Good fitness' due to its distance and elevation, it is also noted as being suitable for all skill levels. This suggests that while stamina is needed, the technical aspects are manageable for less experienced riders.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Puebla de la Reina. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you would typically find parking within the town to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is the Puebla de la Reina — circular por el río Matachel trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not specify whether dogs are allowed on this particular trail. It's always best to check local regulations or signage in Puebla de la Reina before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The trail features a very pleasant path that often runs alongside or through the waters of the Matachel River. The landscape is dynamic, with varied physiography including rivers, brooks, and ravines. You'll encounter diverse scenery, from plains to meadows dotted with ancient holm oaks.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is particularly beautiful in spring when the area blooms after the rains. The landscape changes significantly depending on rainfall, so spring offers a vibrant and picturesque experience. Depending on the season, sections of the Matachel River can be full of water or dry.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: A key highlight is the scenic beauty of the ever-changing natural environment along the Matachel River. You will also pass by the Church of Santa Olalla of Puebla de la Reina, offering a cultural point of interest.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access or ride this mountain bike trail. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Puebla de la Reina, a town where you would typically find local amenities such as cafes, bars, and accommodation options for visitors.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Cañada Real Leonesa Occidental.

waypoints.name: Church of Santa Olalla of Puebla de la Reina
